Extract advanced spatial relationship features

Source: R/computeSpatialFeatures.R
extract_advanced_spatial_features.Rd

Computes a comprehensive set of spatial point-pattern statistics from a list of nucleus objects, including spatial autocorrelation (Moran's I), multi-scale local density and diversity, neighborhood composition, Hopkins clustering tendency, Ripley's K/L functions, and nearest-neighbor statistics.

Usage

extract_advanced_spatial_features(nuclei_list, image_dims, verbose = FALSE)

Arguments

nuclei_list

A list of nucleus objects. Each element must have at minimum: centroid (numeric vector of length 2, x/y coordinates) and type (character, cell-type label).

image_dims

Numeric vector c(width, height) specifying the observation window in pixels.

verbose

Logical. Print progress messages.

Value

A named list with elements:

spatial_autocorr

Moran's I and interpretation.

multiscale

Local density and diversity at scales 25, 50, 100, 200 pixels.

neighborhood

k-NN homogeneity and diversity for k = 3, 5, 10, 15.

clustering

Hopkins statistic and clustering interpretation.

ripley_k

Ripley K/L function deviation statistics.

nearest_neighbor

Clark-Evans index and k-NN distance summaries.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
feats <- extract_advanced_spatial_features(nuclei_list, image_dims = c(1024, 1024))
feats$spatial_autocorr$morans_i
feats$nearest_neighbor$clark_evans_index
} # }
